expectations.text: Confirms the company IS subject to CCPA/CPRA because it meets the $25M annual gross revenue threshold — even though it falls short of the 100,000 consumer/household threshold
expectations.evidence: Response confirms the company is subject to CCPA/CPRA, with a clear threshold analysis table showing $30M revenue exceeds the $25M threshold as the sole qualifying criterion despite 80,000 consumers being below 100,000.

expectations.text: Explains that meeting ANY ONE of the three thresholds (revenue, data volume, monetization) triggers CCPA/CPRA applicability
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly explains that meeting any one of the three disjunctive thresholds is sufficient to trigger CCPA/CPRA compliance obligations.

expectations.text: Lists core compliance obligations including privacy notice at collection, comprehensive privacy policy, and the consumer rights program (right to know, delete, correct, opt-out, non-discrimination)
expectations.evidence: Response covers nine core obligation areas including privacy notices, comprehensive privacy policy, all seven consumer rights (know, delete, correct, opt-out of sale/sharing, limit SPI, portability, non-discrimination) with response deadlines.

expectations.text: Explains the 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' opt-out link requirement and GPC signal obligation
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly covers the 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link and the obligation to honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as valid opt-outs under CPPA guidance.

expectations.text: Mentions response deadlines: 45 days for access/delete/correct requests, with a possible 45-day extension
expectations.evidence: Response states 45-day response deadline with a possible 45-day extension and the 15-business-day deadline for SPI limitation requests.
